title: ColdRelay vs Mailscale — Cold Email Infrastructure Comparison
description: Comparing ColdRelay and Mailscale for cold email infrastructure. See pricing, infrastructure approach, deliverability guarantees, and feature differences side-by-side.

overview.coldrelay: ColdRelay provides dedicated cold email infrastructure with Microsoft 365 mailboxes on isolated Azure tenants with dedicated IPs. Per-mailbox pricing: $1 (1-199), $0.85 (200-999), $0.70 (1K-4,999), $0.55 (5K+). Includes 100-150 mailboxes per domain, auto DNS, no warmup wait, 99% inbox guarantee, and 2-4 hour setup.
overview.competitor: Mailscale is a cold email infrastructure and inbox creation platform that positions itself as 5x cheaper and 50x faster than Google/Microsoft direct. Plans are volume-tiered: Solopreneur at $79/month (~2,000 prospects/month), Business at $119/month (~10,000 prospects), Enterprise at $249/month (~30,000 prospects), and an Unlimited Mailboxes plan for 100,000+ emails/month. Mailscale emphasizes creating 50-1,000 mailboxes in under 60 seconds with instant CSV export, automated DNS, and claims 3,000+ customers. They offer a 7-day free trial and a 'get more replies or don't pay' guarantee.

featureComparison.coldrelay: Per-mailbox: $1-$0.55 — pay per inbox, no tiers
featureComparison.competitor: Tiered by outreach volume: $79-$249/month plus unlimited plan for high volume

featureComparison.coldrelay: Isolated Azure tenants with dedicated IPs — guaranteed per-customer separation
featureComparison.competitor: Infrastructure details less specific — focus on inbox creation speed rather than isolation

featureComparison.coldrelay: Automated provisioning — infrastructure ready in 2-4 hours
featureComparison.competitor: 50-1,000 mailboxes in under 60 seconds with instant CSV export

featureComparison.coldrelay: No warmup required — send from day 1
featureComparison.competitor: Not detailed — platform focuses on mailbox provisioning speed

featureComparison.coldrelay: 99% inbox placement guarantee with money-back promise
featureComparison.competitor: Get more replies or don't pay guarantee — less specific metric

featureComparison.coldrelay: No free trial — covered by money-back guarantee
Growing platform with dedicated infrastructure focus
BYO domains with automated DNS setup
featureComparison.competitor: Buy or add domains inside the platform

featureComparison.coldrelay: Works with Instantly, Smartlead, and other major platforms
featureComparison.competitor: One-click add to email sending tool with CSV export
Fixed plan tiers — need to upgrade as you grow

sections.coldrelayWins: ColdRelay's infrastructure isolation is a clear differentiator — dedicated Azure tenants with dedicated IPs give you control over your sending reputation in a way that platform-based solutions can't match. The 99% inbox placement guarantee is specific and measurable, vs. Mailscale's vaguer 'more replies or don't pay' promise. ColdRelay's per-mailbox volume pricing means your cost per inbox decreases as you scale, while Mailscale's fixed tiers can be constraining. The no-warmup approach saves time and eliminates a common failure point in cold email operations. For serious infrastructure where isolation and deliverability are non-negotiable, ColdRelay provides more confidence.
sections.competitorWins: Mailscale's 60-second mailbox creation with instant CSV export is impressively fast — ideal for teams that need to spin up and down infrastructure frequently. The 7-day free trial removes purchase friction entirely. With 3,000+ customers, Mailscale has significant market validation and likely a more mature platform from a UX standpoint. The tiered plans with fixed monthly costs make budgeting simple for smaller teams. Mailscale's focus on being an all-in-one solution (buy domains, create mailboxes, export to sending tool) reduces the number of tools in your stack.
sections.whoShouldChooseColdRelay: Choose ColdRelay if infrastructure quality, isolation, and deliverability guarantees are your priorities. ColdRelay is best for operations that treat email infrastructure as critical sending infrastructure — agencies managing client campaigns, sales teams with strict deliverability requirements, and large operations benefiting from volume pricing.
sections.whoShouldChooseCompetitor: Choose Mailscale if you want fast mailbox provisioning, a free trial to test before committing, and fixed monthly costs. Mailscale works well for solopreneurs and small teams that want a simple, all-in-one approach to cold email infrastructure without worrying about infrastructure-level details.
sections.verdict: ColdRelay and Mailscale serve different segments of the cold email market. ColdRelay is the infrastructure-focused choice with guaranteed isolation, dedicated IPs, and volume pricing — ideal for serious operations. Mailscale is the speed-focused, all-in-one platform with rapid provisioning and fixed pricing — ideal for smaller teams getting started. For operations where deliverability and infrastructure quality matter most, ColdRelay is the stronger choice.

faqs.question: Can I create mailboxes as fast on ColdRelay as on Mailscale?
faqs.answer: Mailscale creates mailboxes in 60 seconds with CSV export. ColdRelay's infrastructure setup takes 2-4 hours because it provisions dedicated Azure tenants and configures DNS. The extra time reflects a more thorough infrastructure setup process.

faqs.question: Does Mailscale offer dedicated IPs?
faqs.answer: Mailscale doesn't prominently highlight dedicated IP infrastructure. ColdRelay explicitly provides dedicated IPs with isolated Azure tenants for every deployment.

faqs.question: Which is cheaper at 500 mailboxes?
faqs.answer: ColdRelay at $350/month ($0.70/mailbox at 1K tier, or ~$425 at 200-999 tier). Mailscale's Enterprise plan at $249/month covers up to ~30,000 prospects but specific mailbox limits per plan aren't clearly documented. Direct comparison depends on Mailscale's actual mailbox allowances per tier.

faqs.question: Does Mailscale have a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes — Mailscale offers a 7-day free trial on all plans. ColdRelay doesn't offer a free trial but provides a 99% inbox placement guarantee with a money-back promise.

faqs.question: Can I use ColdRelay with Mailscale?
faqs.answer: Not directly — both provide mailbox infrastructure. You'd choose one for infrastructure and pair it with a sending platform (Instantly, Smartlead, etc.). They serve the same role in the cold email stack.
